name: Argentine Federal Savings
description_short: Argentine Federal Savings is a community-focused savings and loan serving the Kansas City area since 1906, offering checking, savings, mortgages, and auto loans with FDIC insurance.
description_long:
Argentine Federal Savings was founded in 1906 as the Argentine Building and Loan Association by local business leaders in the Argentine district of Kansas City, Kansas. The institution originally served the thriving community surrounding the Kansas City Smelting and Refining Company and has operated continuously for over a century. Officially renamed Argentine Savings and Loan in 1955 and Argentine Federal Savings thereafter, the organization has grown from $460,000 in assets to a multi-million dollar institution while maintaining its community-focused mission.

The institution offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business financial products. Their deposit offerings include checking accounts (Economy and Now Checking), savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit with tiered rates. On the lending side, Argentine Federal Savings provides conventional and FHA/VA mortgages with 15-year and 30-year fixed options, adjustable-rate mortgages, home equity lines of credit, auto loans, and specialized rehab loan programs for real estate investors financing up to five residential properties. They also offer small business checking accounts and online banking with mobile access including Apple Pay.

Argenttine Federal Savings distinguishes itself through its explicit community-oriented positioning as a "neighborhood Savings and Loan" serving six generations of Kansas City area families and businesses. Their rehab loan program specifically targets local real estate investors with favorable terms. The institution emphasizes personal service, positioning customers as "valued members" rather than account holders, and maintains physical branch locations in the Kansas City area rather than operating as a purely online entity.

As a traditional savings and loan institution, Argentine Federal Savings operates with geographic limitations to the Kansas City metropolitan area and does not offer services nationally online. Their CD and money market rates shown (4.90% APY for 4-month CDs as of the website snapshot date) reflect rates from 2016 and are outdated. The institution is FDIC-insured and member-owned, providing stability but potentially less competitive rates than larger national banks during certain market conditions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Economy Checking and Now Checking accounts
Statement Savings and Passbook Savings accounts
Money Market accounts with tiered rates
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with promotional rates
Rehab Loan Program for residential properties
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Over 115 years of continuous operation serving Kansas City community (founded 1906)
Specialized rehab loan program for real estate investors financing up to 5 properties
Member FDIC status provides regulatory oversight and consumer protection
Multiple checking account options including small business checking
Offers both conventional and FHA/VA mortgage options with 15 and 30-year terms
Mobile banking and Apple Pay support available for account holders
cons: Limited to Kansas City area service territory; not a national online bank
Website content appears outdated (last update November 2016), rates not current
No mention of competitive rates compared to national banks or credit unions
Limited information on specific account fees, minimum balances, or terms
Auto loan and rehab loan details minimal; no rate information provided for auto loans
best_for: Kansas City area residents seeking traditional community bank with physical branches
Local real estate investors looking for rehabilitation/resale property financing
Families seeking FDIC-insured savings and checking with community-focused service
Small business owners in Kansas City needing business checking accounts
diagnosis: Argentine Federal Savings is best for Kansas City area residents and small business owners seeking traditional community bank services with FDIC insurance and physical branch access. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to the Kansas City area and the lack of current rate and fee information on their website, requiring direct contact with the institution for accurate pricing.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
